mechanism_narrative: Sustained elevated federal funds rate increases the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, reducing equity present values and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are not significantly more negative during high FEDFUNDS regimes than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate above 4% signals weakening labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending forecasts and trigger negative corporate earnings revisions, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If UNRATE crossings above 4% are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and industrial production indicate a coordinated collapse in aggregate demand and manufacturing output, amplifying recession probabilities and producing sharply negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If the joint decline condition does not produce significantly more negative 20-day returns than either proxy alone (interaction term t-stat < 2.0), reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4%, accelerating unemployment forces markets to price an imminent monetary policy pivot toward easing; the resulting discount rate compression outweighs near-term earnings weakness and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If during triggered periods the 60-day forward return is negative in more than 50% of observations, or the interaction term is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05), reject.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production signals contracting manufacturing output and lower corporate revenue growth, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: If month-over-month INDPRO declines are followed by positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of instances over a 3-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales relative to the prior month signal robust consumer spending that supports corporate top-line growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: If positive month-over-month RSAFS changes fail to coincide with positive 20-day SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year window), reject.

mechanism_narrative: Negative nonfarm payroll growth reduces aggregate income and consumption forecasts, leading to downward revisions in corporate earnings and negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS below its 12-month average does not predict negative 60-day forward returns with Rank IC > 0.05 over a 5-year window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: The joint condition of elevated inflation above 300 and restrictive federal funds rates above 4% simultaneously erodes real disposable income and raises corporate discount rates, producing persistently negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If the joint high inflation and high Fed funds condition does not produce negative 60-day returns in at least 60% of triggered instances, reject the hypothesis.
